backend-flow/intro.title: Introduction
backend-flow/intro.description: This tutorial will follow the back-end flow for the ERASICv1. Before starting, three directories should be set up:

backend-flow/io-level/apr.title: Automatic place and route (APR)
backend-flow/io-level/apr.description: The script that runs APR is located at apr/apr.tcl. You will need to calculate the desired X and Y dimensions of the chip, which are called CoreX and CoreY in apr.tcl.

backend-flow/io-level/pad-level-module.title: Pad-level module
backend-flow/io-level/pad-level-module.description: Create a module called DigitalCorePads that instantiates the "logical" top-level (DigitalCore), as well as the I/O pads. You will also need a dummy holder for PCORNER that can be included in the RTL folder of DigitalCorePads. Look at previous code for reference.

backend-flow/io-level/syn.title: Synthesis (SYN)
backend-flow/io-level/syn.description: The script that will run synthesis is located at syn/script/syn.tcl. If you run synthesis as is, the pads will be optimized away because they have no logic connections. In syn.tcl, you have to do setdonttouch for all of your pads:

backend-flow/tapeout-level-final/drc.title: Design rule check (DRC)
backend-flow/tapeout-level-final/drc.description: Locate the following files and copy them into ~/ERASICRev1/APR for ease of use:

backend-flow/tapeout-level-final/gds-exp.title: GDS export
backend-flow/tapeout-level-final/gds-exp.description: Once you are ready to export your design, go to the main Virtuoso window and navigate to File → Export → Stream.

backend-flow/tapeout-level-final/tapeout.title: Tapeout
backend-flow/tapeout-level-final/tapeout.description: To submit your design, you need to fill out a Tapeout Spreadsheet that was provided by MUSE. There are six sheets provided:

backend-flow/tapeout-level-initial/lvs.title: Layout vs. schematic (LVS)
backend-flow/tapeout-level-initial/lvs.description: We want to run LVS with Calibre on the bond-pad level (so no seal ring or dummy fill). Pin labels must be added to the AP level of each pad so that the LVS tool can map the Spice netlist to the layout. They must follow the same naming as ASIC_ALL.sv.

frontend-flow/intro.title: Introduction
frontend-flow/intro.description: This tutorial will follow the front-end flow for the ERASICv1 once I get to it.
